title: Frostbite Station
setting: An abandoned Arctic research station built over a frozen lake. The crew discovered something ancient in the ice that thawed out and now moves in the darkness.

characters.name: Dr. Elara Okafor
characters.description: Lead researcher of Frostbite Station. Her research journal suggests she uncovered something terrifying in the ice core samples.

characters.name: The Watcher in the Ice
characters.description: An ancient entity frozen for millennia, now thawed and stalking the station's corridors. It moves silently, leaving frost patterns and cold spots.

items.name: Flare Torch
items.description: A red emergency flare with a striker wheel. Produces intense heat that can thaw frozen mechanisms.

items.name: Maintenance Keycard (MAINT-04)
items.description: An amber-glowing keycard with frost-damaged edges. Security clearance for maintenance areas.

items.name: Sample Drill
items.description: A heavy-duty coring drill with diamond-tipped bit. Used for extracting ice samples from permafrost.

items.name: Ice Core Artifact
items.description: A cylindrical ice sample containing a dark, organic-looking mass frozen within. Feels unnaturally cold to the touch.

items.name: Dr. Okafor's Research Journal
items.description: A leather-bound journal with frantic handwriting. Contains frequency codes (7.83 Hz, 14.3 Hz) and notes about 'resonance patterns' and 'awakening something'.

items.name: Frequency Transmitter
items.description: A portable device that can broadcast specific low-frequency signals. Used for geological resonance experiments.

items.name: Emergency Fuse
items.description: A heavy ceramic fuse rated for high-voltage systems. Found in the lab's spare parts cabinet.

scenes.name: Landing Pad
scenes.prose: The wind howls across the frozen landing pad, biting through your thermal gear with needles of ice. Frostbite Station looms before you—a cluster of brutalist concrete structures half-buried in snowdrifts, their windows dark and vacant. The main entrance is a steel blast door sealed shut, ice glazing its edges like a frosted tomb. To the north, the corridor entrance waits behind a layer of thick, milky ice that has swollen in the frame. Crates lie overturned, their contents spilled and frozen solid: rations, tools, a scattering of personal effects abandoned in haste. Wedged between two crates, a red cylinder catches your eye—a flare torch, its striker wheel crusted with rime but still functional. The wind carries a sound that isn't wind: a low, resonant hum, felt more than heard, vibrating up through the packed snow under your boots. The station isn't empty. Something is awake inside.

lookDescriptions.flare-torch: A red emergency flare with a metal striker wheel. The label reads 'EXTREME HEAT - USE FOR ICE EMERGENCIES'.
lookDescriptions.crates: Overturned supply crates, contents frozen into indistinguishable lumps. One crate lid is smashed, revealing mangled equipment inside.
lookDescriptions.blast-door: The main station entrance, sealed with thick ice around the edges. No visible handles or controls—likely opened from inside.
lookDescriptions.snowdrifts: Deep, wind-sculpted snow piles against the station walls. Strange, swirling patterns are etched into their surfaces, as if something has moved through them without leaving footprints.

scenes.name: Main Corridor
scenes.prose: Dim red emergency lighting casts long shadows down the corridor, painting everything in the color of old blood. Frost webs across the walls, thick and crystalline, catching the light like frozen capillaries. Three doors branch off: to the west, a door labeled COMM ROOM hangs slightly ajar, darkness pooling beyond; to the east, a sealed door marked LAB glows with the amber light of a card reader, locked; ahead, a heavy reinforced door labeled GENERATOR ROOM vibrates with a low, mechanical pulse. The air is dead-cold and still, smelling of ozone and something organic, like wet soil and decay. A locker has toppled over, spilling jackets and tools. Near it, an amber-glowing keycard lies on the frost-rimed floor—MAINTENANCE-04, dropped in someone's flight. The red light makes the frost patterns on the walls seem to writhe if you stare too long.

lookDescriptions.keycard: An amber keycard labeled MAINT-04. Frost has crept into its chip slot, but the LED still glows weakly.
lookDescriptions.locker: A fallen metal locker, contents spilled: thermal jackets, a broken flashlight, a half‑frozen coffee mug.
lookDescriptions.frost-patterns: Intricate, fern‑like frost crystals covering the walls. They seem to grow thicker near the generator room door.
lookDescriptions.card-reader: The lab door's electronic lock. Amber light pulses slowly, waiting for a valid keycard.

scenes.name: Communications Room
scenes.prose: Banks of dead radio equipment line the walls, their screens dark and dusty. A central console dominates the room, its surface littered with papers—frequency charts, call logs, a handwritten note in a desperate, sprawling script: 'DON'T TRANSMIT BELOW 10 Hz—IT HEARS.' The chair is pushed back as if someone stood up quickly, a thermal mug knocked over, its contents frozen in a brown spill across the floor. On a smaller side table sits a portable frequency transmitter, its display still lit with a soft green glow, showing a standby frequency of 1.00 Hz. A large world map on the wall is dotted with pins marking other research stations, all in the Arctic circle. One pin, red and larger than the rest, marks Frostbite Station. Someone has drawn a circle around it in black marker, and written a single word: 'GROUND ZERO.'

lookDescriptions.frequency-transmitter: A portable device with a digital display and tuning dials. Label: 'RESONANCE EMITTER - GEO‑ACOUSTIC STUDIES'.
lookDescriptions.console: The main communications console. Power is off, but a spare battery pack still has charge.
lookDescriptions.note: A handwritten note: 'DON'T TRANSMIT BELOW 10 Hz—IT HEARS. Okafor ordered full silence. Too late now.'
lookDescriptions.world-map: A map of the Arctic with research stations marked. Frostbite Station is circled in black with 'GROUND ZERO' scrawled beside it.

scenes.name: Laboratory
scenes.prose: The lab is a disaster of shattered glass and overturned equipment. Sample jars lie broken on the floor, their contents—ice cores, geological specimens—scattered and melting into foul‑smelling puddles. In the center of the room, a massive permafrost‑sampling rig stands intact, its drill bit pointed at a section of exposed bedrock wall. The wall shows clear drill marks, and at its center, a cylindrical borehole glistens with fresh ice. On a workbench, a heavy sample drill rests beside a spilled toolbox. A cabinet labeled 'SPARE PARTS' hangs open; inside, among fuses and wiring, you spot a ceramic emergency fuse. Cold radiates from the borehole in waves, and the air tastes metallic, like blood and ozone. Frost creeps across the floor in intricate, spiral patterns, converging on the drill site.

lookDescriptions.sample-drill: A heavy coring drill with a diamond‑tipped bit. The motor is cold but seems operational.
lookDescriptions.permafrost-wall: A section of exposed bedrock with a fresh borehole. Ice glints deep within the hole, something dark suspended inside.
lookDescriptions.spare-parts-cabinet: An open cabinet containing electrical components. The emergency fuse is the largest, rated for 10kV.
lookDescriptions.broken-samples: Shattered ice‑core jars. The melting ice smells wrong—sweet and rotten, like decayed fruit.

scenes.name: Generator Room
scenes.prose: The generator room thrums with latent power. A massive diesel generator occupies the center, its casing frosted over but still emitting a deep, sub‑sonic vibration that shakes the floor. Control panels line the walls, their gauges frozen at zero. One panel has been ripped open, exposing burnt wiring and a missing fuse socket. To the south, a heavy vault door stands sealed, its surface etched with the same swirling frost patterns seen elsewhere, but here they form something almost glyph‑like. A workbench holds tools and a frequency‑coupling unit—a device clearly meant to interface the transmitter with the generator. The air is thick with the smell of diesel and ozone, and the vibration gets into your bones, making your teeth ache. The vault door has no handle, only a small, frosted‑over viewport that shows darkness beyond.

lookDescriptions.generator: A massive diesel generator, frosted over but still producing a low vibration. The missing fuse socket is clearly visible.
lookDescriptions.vault-door: A heavy circular door with frost‑etched glyphs. The viewport is clouded with ice, but something dark moves beyond.
lookDescriptions.frequency-coupler: A rack‑mounted unit with input ports for a transmitter and output leads to the generator.
lookDescriptions.control-panels: Frost‑covered gauges and switches. One panel is smashed, wires hanging out like black intestines.

scenes.name: Frozen Chamber
scenes.prose: The vault door opens onto a vast, natural cavern, its walls sheathed in blue‑white ice that glows with an internal phosphorescence. In the center of the chamber, an ancient device of black stone and frozen metal rises from the ice—a machine of impossible geometry, all sharp angles and non‑Euclidean curves. Before it, a pedestal holds a recessed slot exactly the size of the ice‑core artifact. The air is deathly still and so cold it burns your lungs. The ice walls are not empty: shadows move within them, elongated humanoid shapes frozen mid‑stride, faces pressed against the ice as if trying to break through. The Watcher is here—a presence felt rather than seen, a pressure in the air, a whispering at the edge of hearing. The device hums, awaiting the final key. This is where it all ends, or begins.

lookDescriptions.ancient-device: A structure of black stone and frozen metal, covered in glyphs that swim when you look away. A pedestal before it has a cylindrical slot.
lookDescriptions.ice-walls: Glowing blue ice containing frozen shadows—humanoid shapes trapped forever, their faces distorted in silent screams.
lookDescriptions.pedestal: A stone pedestal with a slot exactly matching the ice‑core artifact. Glyphs around it pulse with faint light.
lookDescriptions.cavern: A vast natural chamber, the ceiling lost in darkness. The ice underfoot is treacherously smooth, and the cold is absolute.

puzzles.result: The flare ignites with a hissing roar, pouring intense heat onto the ice‑sealed door. The ice melts rapidly, steaming away to reveal the corridor entrance. The door groans open, releasing a gust of frigid, stale air.
The keycard clicks home. The amber light flashes green, and the lab door slides open with a whisper of compressed air. Cold, metallic‑scented air washes out from the darkness within.

The drill whines to life, biting into the ice‑filled borehole. With a crack, the core breaks free—a cylinder of clear ice containing a dark, organic mass. As you lift it, a leather‑bound journal, frozen inside the ice, falls to the floor. You now have the ice‑core artifact and Dr. Okafor's research journal.

puzzles.action: install fuse and tune transmitter to 7.83 Hz
puzzles.result: The fuse slots into the generator with a heavy click. The transmitter, tuned to the frequency from the journal, feeds a low hum into the coupling unit. Lights flicker on across the control panels, and the vault door's frost patterns glow with blue light before sliding open with a deep rumble.

puzzles.action: place ice-core artifact in pedestal and activate transmitter
puzzles.result: The artifact slots perfectly into the pedestal. The transmitter, still tuned to the resonance frequency, activates the ancient device. The black stone structure awakens, glyphs burning with cold blue fire. The Watcher's presence intensifies, then dissolves into a sigh of escaping cold. The device falls silent, the threat contained—for now. You have survived Frostbite Station.

winCondition.description: Place the ice‑core artifact into the pedestal and activate the frequency transmitter to resonate with the ancient device, sealing the Watcher back into the ice.
